title: hypericum fallax subsp. immaculatum
intro: Hypericum fallax subsp. immaculatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant species within the Hypericum genus, characterized by its clusters of yellow flowers and red, berry-like fruits. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, St. John's Wort has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and the management of anxiety and depression. The health benefits of St. John's Wort include its potential to reduce symptoms of mild to moderate depression, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and promote relaxation. St. John's Wort can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
